metadata.archetype_justification: Druid is a distributed analytics database whose primary workload is serving read-heavy analytical queries (aggregations, scans, SQL) over pre-ingested data segments. The vast majority of API endpoints are query (GET/POST) operations returning aggregated results. While ingestion writes exist, the runtime role is predominantly a high-throughput data access layer. Classified as data-gateway.
metadata.context: Apache Druid: high-performance real-time analytics database.

top_gaps.title: Network Security
top_gaps.gap: No IaC defining VPC, subnets, or security groups — infrastructure deployment is external to this repo
top_gaps.impact: Deployment security posture is ungoverned from this codebase; no network segmentation evidence

top_gaps.title: Backup and Recovery
top_gaps.gap: No backup configuration, retention policies, or disaster recovery definitions in the repository
top_gaps.impact: Data loss risk if operators do not configure backups externally

top_gaps.title: Audit Logging
top_gaps.gap: No CloudTrail or equivalent audit logging infrastructure defined
top_gaps.impact: Compliance and forensic investigation capabilities are unverifiable

top_gaps.title: SLO Definitions
top_gaps.gap: No SLO definitions, error budgets, or formal service-level targets found
top_gaps.impact: Cannot measure whether system meets user expectations; no prioritization framework for improvements

top_gaps.title: Resource Tagging
top_gaps.gap: No resource tagging governance or tag policies defined
top_gaps.impact: No cost allocation, ownership tracking, or environment identification possible

quick_agent_wins.name: RAG-based Knowledge Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Documentation exists (262 markdown files in /docs/, 29 READMEs, comprehensive API reference). APP-Q5 = 3.
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: A RAG-based knowledge agent that indexes Druid's extensive documentation corpus to answer developer and operator questions about configuration, querying, ingestion, and operations.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Generate OpenAPI specs from the existing REST API annotations (JAX-RS @Path in source code) to enable API tool discovery. Index all /docs/ markdown and API reference content.

quick_agent_wins.name: DevOps Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: CI/CD pipeline exists (INF-Q11 = 3). 13 GitHub Actions workflows cover unit tests, integration tests, Docker builds, CodeQL, and static checks.
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: A DevOps agent that triggers test pipelines, monitors build status, reports coverage regressions, and manages PR automation workflows.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Configure API access tokens for GitHub Actions programmatic triggering.

quick_agent_wins.name: Observability Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Structured metrics emission exists (OPS-Q1 = 3). Druid has OpenTelemetry, Prometheus, Dropwizard, Graphite, StatsD, and InfluxDB emitter extensions. JVM and service status monitors are configured.
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: An observability agent that queries Druid metrics, correlates emitter output with service health, and identifies performance regressions in query latency or ingestion throughput.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Ensure a production deployment has at least one emitter configured (currently defaults to noop).

findings.title: No network security configuration defined
findings.description: No VPC, subnet, security group, NACL, or network segmentation configuration exists within this repository. Infrastructure deployment (including network topology) is handled entirely externally. The Docker Compose exposes all services on host ports without network isolation.
findings.gap: No network security configuration is codified. Deployments depend entirely on external operator/Helm configurations for network isolation.
findings.recommendation: Define VPC architecture as IaC: private subnets for Druid data nodes (Historical, MiddleManager), restricted subnets for coordination (Coordinator, Overlord), and public-facing Router/Broker behind an ALB or API Gateway. Implement security groups with least-privilege rules between service tiers.

findings.title: No managed API entry point
findings.description: Druid exposes HTTP APIs directly on each service (Router on port 8888, Broker on 8082, Coordinator on 8081). The Router service acts as an internal query routing layer but is not an API Gateway — it lacks throttling, authentication enforcement, request validation, and rate limiting at the entry point level.
findings.gap: No managed API entry point with throttling, auth enforcement, or request validation. Services are exposed directly.
findings.recommendation: Place an Application Load Balancer or API Gateway (preferred per preferences) in front of the Router service. Configure throttling, request validation, and WAF integration. Use API Gateway for external query consumers with usage plans and API keys.

findings.title: No auto-scaling configuration
findings.description: No auto-scaling configuration exists within this repository. The Docker Compose defines static container counts. No ASG, ECS auto-scaling, EKS HPA, or KEDA configuration is present.
findings.gap: No auto-scaling definitions for any Druid service tier. Static capacity is the default.
findings.recommendation: Define Kubernetes HPA for Broker and Router services (scale on query latency/CPU). Define cluster autoscaler for Historical nodes (scale on segment storage pressure). Consider KEDA for MiddleManager scaling based on pending task queue depth.

findings.title: No backup and recovery configuration
findings.description: No backup configuration, retention policies, point-in-time recovery settings, or disaster recovery plans exist in this repository. The metadata store (PostgreSQL in Docker Compose) has no backup configuration. Deep storage (local filesystem in default config) has no replication or backup strategy defined.
findings.gap: No automated backup strategy for metadata store or deep storage. No restore procedures documented in the deployment context.
findings.recommendation: Define Aurora PostgreSQL with automated backups and PITR for the metadata store. Configure S3 deep storage with versioning and cross-region replication for segment data. Define AWS Backup plans covering both. Document and test restore procedures.

findings.title: No high availability or multi-AZ configuration
findings.description: No multi-AZ deployment configuration exists within this repository. Docker Compose defines single instances of each service. The Druid architecture supports HA (multiple Historicals, replicated segments, multiple Brokers) but no IaC defines this topology across availability zones.
findings.gap: No multi-AZ configuration. Single-AZ failure would take down the entire cluster as defined in this repo.
findings.recommendation: Define EKS node groups spanning 2+ AZs. Configure Druid segment replication factor >= 2 across AZ-aware Historicals. Deploy Aurora metadata store in Multi-AZ mode. Place ZooKeeper ensemble (or migrate to K8s-native discovery) across 3 AZs.

findings.title: Low infrastructure as code coverage
findings.description: The repository contains Docker/container definitions (Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml) and Kubernetes test manifests but no production IaC. No Terraform, CDK, CloudFormation, or Helm charts for production deployment exist within this repo.
findings.gap: Significant infrastructure is not codified within this repository. Compute, networking, databases, messaging, and operational resources (monitoring, alarms, backups) have no IaC representation here.
findings.recommendation: Create a deploy/ or infrastructure/ directory with Terraform/CDK modules defining: EKS cluster, Aurora PostgreSQL, S3 deep storage, CloudWatch alarms, and AWS Backup plans.

findings.title: Managed compute partially adopted
findings.description: Druid provides a multi-stage Dockerfile producing a distroless JDK17 image. Docker Compose defines a full cluster. Kubernetes extensions exist for K8s-native task execution and service discovery. External deployment is managed via druid-operator on EKS/K8s.
findings.gap: No IaC within this repository defines the managed container orchestration (EKS/ECS) — it is handled externally. The repo provides containerization artifacts but not the orchestration platform definition.
findings.recommendation: Consider co-locating EKS cluster IaC (Terraform/CDK) or at minimum referencing the druid-operator Helm chart as a Git submodule to establish full deployment traceability. Prefer EKS per stated preferences.

findings.title: Managed databases partially adopted
findings.description: Druid's metadata storage supports MySQL, PostgreSQL, and SQL Server via dedicated extensions. Docker Compose uses PostgreSQL 17.6. The druid-aws-rds-extensions module provides IAM authentication for RDS. Production deployments on AWS would use RDS/Aurora.
findings.gap: No IaC defines managed database resources (RDS/Aurora). The repository provides database drivers and connectivity but the infrastructure is provisioned externally.
findings.recommendation: Define Aurora PostgreSQL (preferred) as IaC for the metadata store. Leverage the existing druid-aws-rds-extensions for IAM-based authentication instead of password-based connections.

findings.title: CI automation exists but no CD pipeline
findings.description: Comprehensive CI automation exists: 13 GitHub Actions workflows covering unit tests, integration tests, Docker builds, CodeQL SAST, static analysis, and JaCoCo coverage. However, no CD (deployment) automation exists.
findings.gap: No deployment automation (CD). The pipeline builds and tests but does not deploy. IaC changes are not part of the pipeline either.
findings.recommendation: Add deployment stages: push Docker images to ECR, trigger ArgoCD sync for EKS deployment, include smoke tests post-deployment. Add IaC validation as a PR check when infrastructure code is added.

findings.title: Modular monorepo with shared build
findings.description: Druid is a modular distributed system with 74 Maven modules. Each runtime service is independently deployable. The architecture has well-defined module boundaries. Services communicate via HTTP and ZooKeeper.
findings.gap: While services are independently deployable, they share a single Maven build and a monolithic Docker image. Shared database schemas exist (metadata store).
findings.recommendation: The current modular monorepo structure is appropriate. Consider extracting the web-console into a fully independent build/deploy pipeline. No forced decomposition recommended.

findings.title: API versioning applied but incomplete
findings.description: Druid uses URL-path versioning consistently: /druid/v2/ for query APIs, /druid/coordinator/v1/ for coordination APIs. Deprecated APIs include @Deprecated annotations.
findings.gap: No formal backward compatibility guarantee mechanism or API changelog. No OpenAPI specification generated from JAX-RS annotations.
findings.recommendation: Generate OpenAPI specifications from JAX-RS annotations using a Maven plugin. Establish a formal API deprecation policy with version sunset timelines.

findings.title: Service discovery uses legacy ZooKeeper
findings.description: Druid uses ZooKeeper for service discovery. The kubernetes-extensions module provides K8s-native discovery as an alternative. No hard-coded service endpoints.
findings.gap: ZooKeeper is a legacy coordination service with significant operational overhead. The K8s-native discovery extension exists but is not the default.
findings.recommendation: For EKS deployments (preferred), migrate to the kubernetes-extensions module for service discovery, eliminating ZooKeeper dependency.

findings.title: Deep storage defaults to local filesystem
findings.description: Druid's deep storage supports S3, HDFS, Azure Blob, and GCS. However, the default configuration uses local filesystem. S3 is available as an extension but not the default. No document parsing capabilities.
findings.gap: Default deep storage is local filesystem. S3 integration exists but requires explicit configuration. No document parsing capabilities.
findings.recommendation: Configure S3 as the default deep storage for all production deployments.

findings.title: Data access mostly centralized
findings.description: Druid has a centralized data access architecture: all queries go through the SQL layer or native JSON query API. Metadata access is centralized through the metadata storage connector abstraction.
findings.gap: Some internal modules directly access metadata tables rather than going through the service API layer. SQL layer and native query API are separate access paths.
findings.recommendation: Continue consolidating data access through the SQL layer as the primary interface.

findings.title: Database versions pinned but no update procedure
findings.description: Database driver versions are explicitly pinned: MySQL 8.2.0, PostgreSQL 42.7.2. Docker Compose uses PostgreSQL 17.6. None are at or past EOL.
findings.gap: No documented version-update procedure with downtime windows, rollback plans, and risk acknowledgment.
findings.recommendation: Document a database engine version update procedure. Add version validation to CI that flags when pinned versions approach EOL.

findings.title: No audit logging infrastructure
findings.description: No CloudTrail, audit trail, or immutable logging infrastructure is defined within this repository. Druid has application-level request logging but no audit-grade logging with immutability guarantees.
findings.gap: No audit logging infrastructure. Application logs exist but are not immutable or compliance-grade.
findings.recommendation: Define CloudTrail for API-level audit logging. Configure the request logger with log shipping to S3 with Object Lock enabled. Integrate CloudWatch Logs with retention policies.

findings.title: No encryption at rest configured
findings.description: No encryption-at-rest configuration exists. The default deep storage (local filesystem) and metadata store have no KMS encryption configured. S3 extension supports server-side encryption but no default enforces it.
findings.gap: No encryption at rest for deep storage (segments) or metadata store. No KMS key management.
findings.recommendation: Configure S3 deep storage with SSE-KMS using customer-managed keys. Define Aurora PostgreSQL with KMS encryption enabled. Add KMS key resources to IaC.

findings.title: Authentication available but not enabled by default
findings.description: Druid provides comprehensive authentication extensions (basic, Kerberos, OAuth2/OIDC/SAML) but authentication is disabled by default.
findings.gap: Authentication is not enabled by default. Requires explicit extension loading and configuration for production.
findings.recommendation: Enable druid-basic-security or druid-pac4j (OAuth2/OIDC) by default in production configurations. Consider placing API Gateway with Cognito authorizer in front.

findings.title: Centralized identity available but optional
findings.description: The druid-pac4j extension provides OAuth2/OIDC/SAML integration for SSO. The druid-basic-security extension supports LDAP federation. These are optional extensions.
findings.gap: IdP integration is available but optional. Default deployment has standalone auth (or no auth).
findings.recommendation: Configure Cognito User Pool or corporate IdP integration via the pac4j extension for production deployments.

findings.title: Plaintext passwords in development files
findings.description: Docker Compose environment file contains plaintext password (FoolishPassword). No Secrets Manager or Vault integration is present. The druid-aws-rds-extensions module provides IAM-based authentication.
findings.gap: Development example files contain plaintext passwords. No Secrets Manager integration for production credential management. No rotation mechanism.
findings.recommendation: Integrate AWS Secrets Manager for metadata store credentials. Leverage the existing druid-aws-rds-extensions for IAM authentication to Aurora PostgreSQL.

findings.title: Hardened base image but no automated scanning
findings.description: Production Docker image uses gcr.io/distroless/java17-debian12 (hardened, minimal base). OWASP dependency check suppressions file exists. However, no automated vulnerability scanning pipeline is configured.
findings.gap: Good base image choice but no automated vulnerability scanning in CI/CD pipeline. No ECR image scanning configuration.
findings.recommendation: Enable ECR image scanning. Add OWASP dependency-check or Snyk as a CI pipeline step. Consider AWS Inspector for runtime vulnerability analysis.

findings.title: SAST present but no container scanning or blocking gate
findings.description: CodeQL SAST runs in CI scanning Java, JavaScript, and Python. Static checks workflow includes Maven static analysis. However, no container scanning and no blocking security gate.
findings.gap: No explicit container scanning. No blocking security gate on critical CodeQL findings.
findings.recommendation: Add container scanning (ECR scanning or Trivy) to Docker build workflow. Configure CodeQL to block PRs on critical/high findings. Add dependency-check step.

findings.title: Distributed tracing available but uses older SDK
findings.description: The opentelemetry-emitter extension provides OpenTelemetry span emission. Internal request ID propagation exists. Prometheus, Graphite, StatsD, and InfluxDB emitters are available.
findings.gap: OpenTelemetry support uses older SDK version (1.7.0). It's a contrib extension (optional) and emitter defaults to noop.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade OpenTelemetry emitter to current SDK version. Promote to core extension. Ensure W3C Trace Context propagation across all internal calls. Integrate with AWS X-Ray.

findings.title: No SLO definitions
findings.description: No SLO definitions, error budgets, or formal service-level targets found. No CloudWatch alarm definitions for latency. Metrics framework exists but no SLOs are defined on top of them.
findings.gap: No SLOs defined for query latency, ingestion throughput, availability, or any critical user journey. No error budget framework.
findings.recommendation: Define SLOs for: query p99 latency (<500ms), ingestion lag (<30s), metadata store availability (99.9%), and segment availability (99.99%). Implement as CloudWatch composite alarms with error budget dashboards.

findings.title: Business metrics available but not published by default
findings.description: Druid publishes custom operational/business metrics via its emitter framework. These go beyond infrastructure metrics to measure query performance and ingestion throughput.
findings.gap: Default emitter is noop — metrics are not published unless explicitly configured. No pre-built dashboards.
findings.recommendation: Create CloudWatch dashboard templates for key business metrics. Enable Prometheus or CloudWatch emitter by default.

findings.title: No alerting or anomaly detection defined
findings.description: The metrics infrastructure supports alerting but no alert definitions, CloudWatch alarms, or anomaly detection configurations exist within this repository.
findings.gap: No alerting or anomaly detection defined. Metrics are available but no rules consume them.
findings.recommendation: Define CloudWatch alarms for query error rate, p99 latency, ingestion lag, and segment unavailability. Enable CloudWatch anomaly detection on key metrics.

findings.title: No canary or blue/green deployment strategy
findings.description: Docker image builds exist with basic health check support. No canary, blue/green, or rolling deployment strategy is configured within this repository.
findings.gap: No deployment strategy beyond basic Docker image builds. No canary or blue/green deployments. No traffic shifting or automated rollback.
findings.recommendation: Implement rolling deployments with health checks via druid-operator on EKS. For critical deployments, implement canary traffic shifting.

findings.title: Integration testing exists with some gaps
findings.description: Comprehensive integration testing exists: embedded-tests module with Testcontainers, Docker-based full cluster tests, cron-scheduled integration tests, quidem SQL correctness tests.
findings.gap: Integration tests cover query correctness but may not cover all critical operational workflows (failover, scaling, upgrade scenarios).
findings.recommendation: Add integration tests for metadata store failover, segment replication recovery, discovery failover, and rolling upgrade scenarios.

findings.title: No incident response automation
findings.description: No runbooks, SSM Automation documents, Lambda-based remediation, or self-healing patterns exist. The /docs/operations/ directory has human-readable guides only.
findings.gap: No automated incident response. No runbooks in any form. Incident response is entirely ad hoc.
findings.recommendation: Create runbooks for common incidents: segment unavailability, ingestion lag, metadata store connection failure, ZooKeeper loss. Implement as SSM Automation or Step Functions.

findings.title: No observability ownership model
findings.description: Metrics monitors exist per service type but no CODEOWNERS for observability assets, no named alarm owners, no team-specific dashboards.
findings.gap: Observability infrastructure exists but has no ownership model. No team attribution.
findings.recommendation: Define CODEOWNERS for observability configurations. Create per-service-tier dashboards with named team ownership.

findings.title: No resource tagging governance
findings.description: No resource tagging configuration, tag policies, or tagging standards exist. No IaC exists to apply tags to. Docker images have a maintainer label but no AWS resource tags.
findings.gap: No resource tagging governance. No cost allocation, ownership tracking, or environment identification possible.
findings.recommendation: Define a tagging standard (environment, service-tier, team, cost-center) and enforce via IaC default_tags. Implement AWS Tag Policies via Organizations.

pathways.name: Move to Cloud Native
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: APP-Q2 = 3 (already modular); primary trigger not met

triggering_questions.note: APP-Q2 = 3 meets threshold; monolith decomposition not needed
INF-Q1 = 3 meets threshold; managed compute partially adopted

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1 = 3, Dockerfiles exist; compute already containerized

triggering_questions.note: INF-Q1 = 3 meets threshold; containerization already in place

pathways.not_triggered_reason: Workload is already containerized with Dockerfile and Kubernetes extensions. Container pathway does not apply.

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4 = 4 (no stored procedures); Druid itself is open source

triggering_questions.note: DATA-Q4 = 4 (pass); no proprietary SQL or commercial DB engines detected

pathways.name: Move to Managed Databases
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q2 = 3; metadata storage supports RDS PostgreSQL/MySQL

triggering_questions.note: INF-Q2 = 3 meets threshold; managed database support exists
DATA-Q3 = 3 meets threshold; versions pinned and not EOL

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q4 = 4 (data-gateway archetype, sync reads are correct); Druid IS the analytics platform

triggering_questions.note: INF-Q4 = 4 (pass); archetype-calibrated as data-gateway, sync reads correct

pathways.not_triggered_reason: Druid itself is the analytics platform. Managed analytics migration is not applicable — the system provides analytics, it does not consume analytics services.

triggering_questions.note: INF-Q10 < 3 — low IaC coverage triggers pathway

detail.current_state: No production IaC exists. CI is comprehensive (13 workflows) but no CD pipeline. No deployment strategy beyond Docker builds.
immediate_actions: Create IaC directory with EKS cluster and Aurora PostgreSQL definitions
Add CD stages to GitHub Actions (ECR push, ArgoCD sync)
Define CloudWatch alarms and AWS Backup plans as IaC

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent/LLM intent detected in portfolio or service context. The context describes Apache Druid as a 'high-performance real-time analytics database' with no AI-related signal terms.
